executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware attack wave affecting at least 100 organizations across all sectors in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (retail), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. Twenty distinct ransomware groups are actively targeting Canadian infrastructure, with Qilin (23 victims), Play (11 victims), Safepay (11 victims), and Clop (13 victims) leading the assault. This represents a coordinated campaign against Canadian critical infrastructure spanning healthcare, education, government, and essential services.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 23 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products of Canada, Makivik, ALLMAX, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including briwaycarriers.com, mattandsteve.com, ripobec.com, groupepiche.ca, precisionaluminum.ca, kenalex.ca, silverlinegroupinc.com, teccart.qc.ca (education), mactavishco.ca
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Clop

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under siege with 100+ organizations compromised by ransomware in 48 hours across all sectors. Critical infrastructure including public health agencies, municipalities, schools, telecommunications, and healthcare facilities have been breached. No official CCCS advisories were identified in the current feed, suggesting the scale of this incident may still be developing.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major Canadian retail brand) compromised by Coinbasecartel ransomware group. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash financial services hit by Lynx. Multiple accounting/CPA firms breached including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and reidhurstnagy.com.

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940) is being actively exploited in the wild, affecting 1.5 million servers worldwide. Canadian government (CCCS) has issued four advisories in the past two weeks covering Linux kernel exploits, Cisco device vulnerabilities, and targeted social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS platforms. Supply chain attacks are surging across npm, Ruby Gems, and Go modules attributed to TeamPCP, while ShinyHunters continues a coordinated data breach campaign hitting luxury and financial services brands. A trivial Linux kernel exploit (CVE-2026-31431) allows root access with publicly available exploit code, catching defenders flat-footed.

items.description: Critical authentication bypass in cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) allowing unauthenticated access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Trivial exploit allowing root access to Linux kernel, effectively unpatched
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Apache path traversal vulnerability being scanned by UK-based IP address
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: npm, Ruby Gems, Go Modules - cross-platform supply chain compromise affecting six ecosystems
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1552.001 (Credentials In Files)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Towerpoint Wealth LLC, Aman luxury hotels, Marcus & Millichap real estate, Follett Software (EdTech) - coordinated wave hitting 8+ organizations in April 2026
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) - exploiting Salesforce misconfigurations

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Zinkan & Barker Development, Eduporium (educational services), multiple unverified claims
items.ttps: T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1087 (Account Discovery), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - stealthy RDP history enumeration to map victim networks

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: Using AI for cybersecurity and cryptocurrency targeting
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - industrializing cyber attacks with AI

items.name: 313 Team (Pro-Iran)
items.targets: Ubuntu.com - DDoS attack turning outage into propaganda
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1583 (Acquire Infrastructure)

items.name: CORDIAL SPIDER / SNARKY SPIDER
items.targets: Enterprise SaaS environments - high-speed attacks exploiting SSO and AiTM
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1556 (Modify Authentication Process) - rapid data exfiltration via SSO abuse

items.name: PromptLock Ransomware
items.targets: Unknown - first AI-powered ransomware using local OpenAI model through Ollama
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - uses AI to generate malicious payloads

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued four critical advisories in the past two weeks covering cPanel authentication bypass (CVE-2026-41940), Linux kernel exploits (CVE-2026-31431), Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities, and social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS environments. A Senate report warns that Russia is targeting Canada with disinformation campaigns. No major Canadian retail or financial institution breaches were identified in this period.
